text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers examine each other's code, they can catch bugs early—long before they reach production. This saves both time and frustration. Beyond finding errors, code reviews also promote knowledge sharing within the team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, and everyone stays familiar with different parts of the codebase. Over time, this improves overall team quality. Rather than being a criticism, code reviews should be seen as a collaborative tool that builds trust and raises standards across the team.
